trivia_bullets.text: The 'Wumpus' creature lineage in MTG traces to Hunted Wumpus (Mercadian Masques, 1999), a 6/6 for {3}{G} with a free-creature-for-opponents drawback. Shivan Wumpus (Planar Chaos, 2007) followed with a similar pattern. Wumpus Aberration is the third and the first to be typed Eldrazi — its conditional drawback (opponent may deploy a hand creature unless {C} was used) is an Eldrazi-era adaptation of the original Wumpus design.

"All who behold its twisted form are consumed by a single-minded obsession: destroy it." [Scryfall]. No attributed speaker — the line reads as an in-world observational report, implying the creature's wrongness triggers almost involuntary aggression in any observer.

A 6/6 Eldrazi Beast with Trample and Devoid for {3}{G}. The drawback is negated if a colorless {C} mana source was spent to cast it, incentivizing colorless mana production. EDHREC rank 19,422 places it at the low end of Commander use; competitive play is minimal.
Illustrated by Filip Burburan, a Croatia-based MTG illustrator known for creature and horror-register work. No Hipsters of the Coast artist spotlight found for Burburan as of May 2026.

related_cards.name: Hunted Wumpus
related_cards.note: 1999 originator of the Wumpus 'opponent gets a free creature' drawback pattern

related_cards.name: Emrakul, the Promised End
related_cards.note: Apex Eldrazi with an adversarial design philosophy; same Devoid register
